skill.name: azure-ai-projects-java
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure AI Projects SDK for Java to manage Azure AI Foundry project resources.
skill.summary: Use Java examples for Azure AI Foundry project clients, indexes, datasets, and evaluations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and critical combination findings are false positives caused by Markdown fences, inline code, and documentation text. The skill contains Java SDK usage guidance with placeholder Azure endpoints and environment variable examples, with no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Documentation Includes Azure Endpoint and Reference URLs
low_findings.description: Static network findings are hardcoded documentation URLs and a placeholder Azure project endpoint, not active network requests. This is safe in context, but users should replace placeholders with their own Azure resource values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a placeholder endpoint and Microsoft or Azure GitHub reference links. No code in the skill performs network transmission.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Examples Are Configuration Only
low_findings.description: The skill reads PROJECT_ENDPOINT, AI_SEARCH_CONNECTION_NAME, and AI_SEARCH_INDEX_NAME in Java examples. These values configure Azure clients and indexes; no evidence shows secret collection or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variables are named configuration values used directly in SDK builder calls. The file contains no external sink for those values.

low_findings.title: Static External Command and Weak Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences or inline code formatting. The weak cryptography matches occur in descriptive text, not cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows Markdown fences and ordinary documentation text at representative flagged locations. There is no Ruby code, shell execution, or weak cryptographic algorithm call.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Critical Combination Heuristic
dangerous_patterns.description: The critical heuristic combined code fence markers, documentation links, and configuration examples. Manual review found no executable payload, no hidden network call, and no credential exfiltration path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is a Markdown guide with Java examples and reference links. No evidence found for obfuscation, prompt injection, or malicious control flow.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Projects Java Apps
content.value_statement: Azure AI Foundry project setup can be difficult when client roles and SDK entry points are unclear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Java guidance for clients, indexes, datasets, and evaluations.
actual_capabilities: Shows Maven dependency setup for the Azure AI Projects Java SDK.
Explains how to create an AIProjectClientBuilder with DefaultAzureCredential.
Lists available sub-clients for connections, datasets, deployments, indexes, evaluations, evaluators, and schedules.
Provides Java examples for listing connections and indexes.
Shows how to create or update an Azure AI Search-backed index.
Points users to Microsoft documentation, API reference, source, and samples.
limitations: Does not include a runnable Java project or automated tests.
Uses beta SDK examples that may change as Azure packages evolve.
Requires an existing Azure AI Foundry project and valid Azure authentication.
Does not cover every SDK model, permission, or deployment scenario.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Foundry Java Client
use_cases.description: Create a basic Java client setup for an Azure AI Foundry project using the documented builder pattern.

use_cases.title: Review Project Resource Access
use_cases.description: Understand which SDK sub-client handles connections, datasets, deployments, indexes, evaluations, and schedules.

use_cases.title: Prepare Index Management Code
use_cases.description: Draft Java code that links Azure AI Projects indexes to Azure AI Search connection names and index names.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up the SDK
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the Maven dependency and minimal Java client setup for Azure AI Projects using DefaultAzureCredential.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain which Azure AI Projects Java sub-client I should use for connections, datasets, indexes, deployments, and evaluations.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Index Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write Java code to create or update an Azure AI Projects index backed by an Azure AI Search index.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure AI Projects Java integration plan for authentication, pagination, error handling, environment variables, and SDK limitations.

output_examples.input: I need to connect a Java app to my Azure AI Foundry project.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline the Maven package, PROJECT_ENDPOINT configuration, DefaultAzureCredential setup, and client builder pattern.

output_examples.input: I need to list project connections and indexes.
output_examples.output: The skill would describe using ConnectionsClient and IndexesClient, including pagination and the fields to inspect.

output_examples.input: I need to prepare an index update workflow.
output_examples.output: The skill would guide you through index name, version, Azure AI Search connection name, and search index name inputs.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential with properly scoped Azure identities.
Store project endpoint and connection names in environment-specific configuration.
Handle pagination and Azure HTTP exceptions in production workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode real Azure resource secrets in source files.
Do not assume beta SDK APIs will remain stable without checking current documentation.
Do not print sensitive credential values when inspecting project connections.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps users apply the Azure AI Projects Java SDK to common Azure AI Foundry project tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Java code?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples for users to adapt in their own projects.

faq.question: Which authentication method does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends DefaultAzureCredential, which supports several Azure identity flows.

faq.question: Does it include Azure AI Search index examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how an Azure AI Projects index can reference an Azure AI Search connection and index.

faq.question: Can it help with evaluations?
faq.answer: Yes. It identifies the evaluations client and notes access to OpenAI evaluation APIs through the SDK.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples and benign reference links, not malicious behavior.
